input_natural_language: The autopilot shall change states from MANEUVER to STANDBY when the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good.
explanation:
The LTL formula is composed of two parts.

The first part captures the condition: 'whenever the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, autopilot changes from MANEUVER to STANDBY'. This means that if standby and good are true, the second part must also be true.

The second part captures the requirement: 'the autopilot shall change states from MANEUVER to STANDBY'. Specifically, whenever the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state, it will eventually satisfy the condition of being in the STANDBY state.
decision1_substring: when the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good
output_structured_natural_language: whenever standby & good, eventually satisfy state_is_STANDBY
